title: Blue/Green Deployment
description: Two environments; switch traffic; fast rollback; higher infra cost.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Blue/Green deployment requires running TWO complete production environments simultaneously. Min ($100) represents a small application with 2x t3.small instances ($15/mo each), 2x small databases ($30/mo each), and basic load balancer ($20/mo). Max ($6000) represents production workload with 2x multi-instance clusters (20 instances @ $100/mo = $2000 per environment), 2x managed database clusters ($800/mo each), 2x load balancers with high traffic ($200/mo each), and increased data transfer/storage costs. The 2x multiplier is fundamental to this pattern - you're paying for duplicate infrastructure. During transition periods, both environments are active. Cost can be optimized by using scaled-down blue environment between deployments, but true blue/green maintains parity.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity deployment pattern. Requires: (1) Infrastructure-as-Code templates for duplicate environments (8-16 hours), (2) Automated traffic switching mechanism with load balancer/DNS configuration (8-12 hours), (3) Health check and validation automation (4-8 hours), (4) Rollback procedures and runbooks (4-6 hours), (5) Database migration strategy for stateful components (8-12 hours - complex consideration), (6) Monitoring and alerting for both environments (4-8 hours), (7) CI/CD pipeline integration (8-12 hours). Total 44-74 engineering hours at $100-150/hour blended rate. Requires understanding of infrastructure orchestration, networking, and deployment automation. Lower than microservices complexity but higher than simple deployment patterns due to dual-environment coordination and state management challenges.
provenance.licenseCost: Blue/Green deployment is a methodology, not a licensed product. Can be implemented using free/open-source tools: AWS Elastic Load Balancer (usage-based, included in runtime costs), Kubernetes with native deployment strategies (free), Terraform/CloudFormation (free), Jenkins/GitLab CI (free/open-source editions), or cloud-native deployment services (AWS CodeDeploy, Azure Deployment Slots - free services with usage-based pricing). Commercial CI/CD platforms (Harness, Spinnaker Enterprise) that simplify blue/green cost $10k-50k/year but aren't required. Setting to $0 as pattern is achievable with OSS tooling.

provides.reasoning: Blue/Green deployment enables switching traffic between environments without downtime, which is the primary benefit of this pattern.
Rolling back is instantaneous by switching traffic back to the previous environment, which is a core advantage of maintaining two environments.
Testing in production-like environment before switching traffic reduces release risk, making deployments safer.

requires.reasoning: The pattern fundamentally depends on the ability to route traffic between blue and green environments, typically via load balancer or DNS.
Blue and green environments must be isolated to prevent interference, requiring infrastructure that can maintain separate identical environments.
Managing database changes across environments is a critical consideration for stateful applications, requiring backward-compatible schemas or sophisticated migration approaches.

supports_nfr.reason: Blue/green deployment is only required when availability target is up to 99%.
Blue/green deployment achieves up to 99.99% availability through zero-downtime traffic switching.
Fast rollback by switching traffic back to blue environment typically completes in 2+ minutes.
Blue/green requires full environment provisioning per deployment. At >1800 deploys/hour (>0.5/minute), environment switching overhead becomes a bottleneck.
Pattern is GDPR-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is CCPA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is HIPAA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling and provider BAAs, not this pattern.
Pattern is SOX-agnostic; compliance depends on audit controls in the application layer, not this pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Blue/green deployment is a universal deployment strategy implementable across all cloud providers and on-premises infrastructure.
Blue/green deployment is an infrastructure pattern independent of application programming language.
Blue/green deployment applies to server-side platforms where traffic can be routed between environments.
Blue/green deployment works with or without caching layers, though cache warming may be required.
Blue/green deployment is compatible with synchronous and asynchronous messaging architectures.
Blue/green deployment supports AI inference workloads and non-AI workloads equally.
Blue/green deployment is compatible with single-tenant and multi-tenant applications.
Blue/green deployment supports batch processing workloads with appropriate job coordination during cutover.
Blue/green deployment does not constrain distributed transaction patterns, though shared backend data stores are required.
Blue/green deployment supports real-time streaming applications with appropriate connection management during cutover.
Blue/green deployment is compatible with applications using vector search capabilities.
Blue/green deployment works with document stores and other database types equivalently.
Blue/green deployment is compatible with key-value stores and other persistence mechanisms.
Blue/green deployment supports applications using graph databases without constraint.
Blue/green deployment is compatible with time-series databases and other data stores.
Blue/green deployment supports OLTP workloads with shared database backend across environments.
Blue/green deployment is compatible with OLAP workloads and analytical processing applications.

traffic_switch_strategy.description: How traffic shifts from blue to green. Instant: immediate 100% switch (fastest, higher risk). Gradual: percentage-based rollout (balanced). Canary: small test group first (safest, slowest).

health_check_required.description: Whether green environment must pass health checks before traffic switch. True: safer but slower deployment. False: faster but riskier.

rollback_strategy.description: How to handle failures. Automatic: instant rollback on errors (safest). Manual: requires human approval (controlled). Disabled: no rollback capability (not recommended).

environment_parity.description: Green environment sizing. Full: identical to blue (highest cost, zero performance impact). Scaled-down: reduced capacity (medium cost, may need warm-up). Minimal: basic validation only (lowest cost, requires scaling before switch).

warm_up_period_minutes.description: Time to warm up green environment (cache priming, connection pools, JIT compilation) before switching traffic. 0 for instant switch, 5-15 typical for most apps.
